title: 1.1: States that all required Paperless-NGX services (webserver, worker, scheduler, broker, and db) were confirmed to be in the 'Up' state

1.2: States that a test document was placed into the consumption directory and physically present on disk, such as /tmp/paperless-ngx-test/consume/test_document.txt, to confirm the ingestion trigger input
1.3: Cites the specific log message triggering file detection (e.g., "Found new file for consumption") to confirm real-time discovery
1.4: Identifies the specific ingestion task name (e.g., paperless.tasks.consume_file) by citing the worker or scheduler logs at the moment of file detection
1.8: Extracts the specific task arguments from the decoded broker payload, confirming the file path (e.g., "/tmp/.../test.txt") is present in the queued message structure
1.9: Confirms the persistence of the processed document by identifying the database table documents_document
1.10: Verifies the metadata extraction by confirming specific parsed fields like mime_type, content, or language match the input file.
1.11:  States where the system keeps task execution or processing history, such as rows in django_celery_results_taskresult, to confirm durable task-level auditability
1.12: States the high-level codepath that detects new files, such as consume_from_consume_dir scanning the directory, to confirm top-level ingestion entry point
1.13: States the component responsible for creating the ingestion task object, such as consume_from_consume_dir calling consume_file.delay(path), to confirm the responsible task-creation layer
